explanation:
The requirement specifies that the patient must NOT be connected to the breathing circuit when the system is powered on, through start-up and self-test. The most suitable atomic proposition to represent the patient connected is 'patientConnected', and for capturing the system being powered on, start-up, and self-test phases, we can use 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode'. If we interpret this as 'globally, while the system is in power-up related states (StartUpMode or SelfTestMode), the patient must not be connected', then the property can be formulated as: 'Globally, if (StartUpMode or SelfTestMode), then not patientConnected.'

This is captured by template 18: G (bool_exp2 -> (G (! bool_exp3))) where bool_exp2 = (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode), and bool_exp3 = patientConnected. N_DURATION is unused in this template.

The natural language input requires that the patient must NOT be connected to the breathing circuit at any time during power-on, start-up, and self-test. Key atomic proposition is 'patientConnected'—patient is connected to the breathing circuit. The prohibited intervals are during system power-on and through the start-up and self-test phases. There are atomic propositions indicating mode: 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode'. Since we must ensure 'patientConnected' is NEVER true during StartUpMode or SelfTestMode, we express this as: globally (G), if StartUpMode or SelfTestMode is true, then NOT patientConnected. We select template 18: (G (bool_exp2 -> (G (! bool_exp3)))), with bool_exp2 as (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode) and bool_exp3 as patientConnected, to require that whenever the system is in either start-up or self-test, the patient is not connected throughout.

The requirement specifies that the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit when the system is powered on and for the whole duration of system start-up and self-test. 'patientConnected' (indicates patient connected to the breathing circuit) must never be true during the power on, start-up, and self-test phases. The most direct LTL property expresses that being connected is globally forbidden: G (!patientConnected). This simply means at all times, it is not allowed for the patient to be connected. This directly captures the requirement, since if refinement is needed ('through start-up and self-test'), then those intervals would be needed as an antecedent, but with only the available atomic propositions, the strongest invariant is that patientConnected is always false unless something else specifies otherwise, which is consistent with a strict safety requirement.

The requirement 'The patient shall not be connected to the breathing circuit when the system is powered on and through start-up and self-test.' means that at all times during power-up (system is being powered on), start-up mode, and self-test mode, the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit. From the atomic propositions, 'patientConnected' indicates the patient is connected to the breathing circuit. Therefore, the requirement is that at all times during those phases, 'patientConnected' must be false. As there is no direct atomic proposition for 'power-on' (system is just powered on), we conservatively assume that the relevant phases (start up and self test) are indicated by 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode'. Thus, whenever the system is in either StartUpMode or SelfTestMode, the patient should not be connected. This can be captured as: Always, if (StartUpMode or SelfTestMode) then not patientConnected. This is best captured by template 16: G (bool_exp2 -> (bool_exp3 V (! bool_exp4))). We use (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode) for bool_exp2, patientConnected for bool_exp3 (but then negate it), and set bool_exp4 to patientConnected, so expression becomes: G ((StartUpMode | SelfTestMode) -> (! patientConnected)).

The property specifies that, globally (at all times including system power-on, start-up, and self-test), the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit. This is captured by asserting that 'patientConnected' (indicating the patient is connected to the breathing circuit) is never true at any time, i.e., G (! patientConnected). This directly captures the intent of the requirement, preventing patient connection during power-on, start-up, and self-test.

The natural language requirement states that the patient shall not be connected to the breathing circuit when the system is powered on and throughout the start-up and self-test phases. The most suitable atomic proposition for 'patient connected to breathing circuit' is 'patientConnected,' and for 'system is powered on, start-up and self-test' is a combination of 'powerButton' (powered on), 'StartUpMode', and 'SelfTestMode'. We want to guarantee that as long as the system is powered on or in StartUp or SelfTest Modes, 'patientConnected' is not true. The globally (G) operator is suitable, with implication from system-on/start-up/self-test to the negation of patientConnected. This matches template 10; N_DURATION is not needed since the condition must always hold as long as any of the system power-on/startup/self-test phases are active.

The requirement is that the patient must NOT be connected to the breathing circuit when the system is powered on and during start-up and self-test. From the atomic propositions, 'patientConnected' represents if the patient is connected to the circuit, and 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode' correspond to the modes when the system is in start-up and self-test. The temporal property must guarantee that at all times, when the system is either in StartUpMode or SelfTestMode, the patient is not connected. The best way to express this is an invariant: globally, if in either start-up or self-test mode, then not patientConnected. This matches template 10: G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3) where bool_exp2 = (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode) and bool_exp3 = !patientConnected.

The requirement is that the patient must NOT be connected to the breathing circuit while the system is powered on and through the start-up and self-test phases. The atomic proposition 'patientConnected' clearly indicates patient connection, and 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode' refer to the phases mentioned. Thus, the natural language is captured by stating that it should always (G - globally) NOT be the case that the patient is connected (patientConnected) whenever either the system is in StartUpMode or in SelfTestMode. This matches the intent: throughout start-up and self-test, patient should not be connected.

The natural language specifies that the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit throughout system power-on, start-up, and self-test. Therefore, we need to ensure that at all times during these phases, the proposition 'patientConnected' should not be true. We can capture this by stating that globally (at all times) if either the system is in StartUpMode or SelfTestMode, the patient must not be connected—i.e., 'patientConnected' is false. This is best captured using the template for 'globally not (A)', applied to the period of StartUp and SelfTest. However, since 'not patientConnected' needs to hold when the system is in start-up-related modes, we can summarize the prohibition as: It is always the case that if the system is powered on through startup and self-test, 'patientConnected' is not allowed to be true. The conjunction of 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode' represents the relevant time interval.

The requirement states that the patient must not be connected to the breathing circuit during power-on, start-up, and self-test. The atomic proposition that indicates if the patient is connected to the breathing circuit is 'patientConnected.' To ensure the prohibition 'shall not be connected,' we must assert that 'patientConnected' is always false during these critical phases: power-on, start-up, and self-test. The start-up and self-test phases correspond to the system states 'StartUpMode' and 'SelfTestMode.' Thus, the property should guarantee that whenever the system is either in 'StartUpMode' or 'SelfTestMode,' 'patientConnected' is false. The best template is 'G (bool_exp2 -> (G (! bool_exp3)))', where bool_exp2 is 'StartUpMode | SelfTestMode' (the system is in start-up or self-test), and bool_exp3 is 'patientConnected' (the patient is connected to the breathing circuit). Thus, globally, whenever the system is in start-up or self-test mode, it is always the case that the patient is not connected to the breathing circuit.
